justification: All pass criteria met: optimistic locking via updateSeatStatus with expectedCurrentStatus='available'; false return throws SEAT_ALREADY_RESERVED; AC3 tested with mock returning false and Promise.allSettled verifying exactly one success/one failure; AC4 tested with findSeat returning null -> SEAT_NOT_FOUND; acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 4 ACs. The only minor gap is that the confidence section doesn't explicitly call out that AC3 requires integration/concurrency testing to fully verify beyond unit-level mocks, but the AC3 notes do acknowledge the optimistic lock mechanism and the tests simulate the race condition correctly.

observations.text: Optimistic locking correctly implemented with expectedCurrentStatus='available' parameter
AC3 test uses Promise.allSettled to simulate concurrent calls with mock returning false on second attempt
Fast-path optimization on AC2 avoids unnecessary write when seat already reserved
All 4 ACs mapped in acceptance_criteria_coverage with detailed notes
ReservationError typed class is a sensible deviation that improves DX without breaking spec
Confidence section does not explicitly acknowledge that AC3 cannot be fully verified with unit tests alone — the concurrency testing limitation is implied but not stated
